Solving this problem is challenging due to the combinatorial explosion of possible path combinations together with the temporal nature of the problem (time needs to be accounted for as well). To address this, we propose a nested branch-and-bound framework that efficiently explores the space of robot paths in a hierarchical manner. We empirically evaluate our algorithm and demonstrate a speedup of up to two orders of magnitude when compared to a baseline approach.
tldr: The paper introduces a nested branch-and-bound framework for the Joint Task Assistance Planning problem, where a task robot is assisted by another robot providing sensor-based support, and the goal is to maximize assistance duration. They show significant speedups compared to a baseline.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种嵌套分支定界框架，用于解决联合任务辅助规划问题，其中一个任务机器人由另一个提供基于传感器的支持的机器人辅助，目标是最大化辅助持续时间。他们展示了与基线相比的显著加速。
summary_zh: 我们引入并研究联合任务辅助规划问题，该问题推广了先前关于优化机器人协作中辅助的研究工作。在该场景中，两个机器人在预定义的路线图上运行，每个路线图都表示为其配置空间对应的图。其中一个机器人，即任务机器人，必须执行一个定时的任务，而另一个机器人，即辅助机器人，提供基于传感器的支持，这种支持取决于它们之间的空间关系。目标是计算两个机器人的路径，以最大化总辅助时长。由于可能的路径组合的组合爆炸，以及问题的时间性质（时间也需要考虑），解决此问题具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个嵌套的分支定界框架，以分层方式有效地探索机器人路径空间。我们通过实验评估了我们的算法，并证明了与基线方法相比，速度提升高达两个数量级。

title: Flow4R: Unifying 4D Reconstruction and Tracking with Scene Flow
summary: Reconstructing and tracking dynamic 3D scenes remains a fundamental challenge in computer vision. Existing approaches often decouple geometry from motion: multi-view reconstruction methods assume static scenes, while dynamic tracking frameworks rely on explicit camera pose estimation or separate motion models. We propose Flow4R, a unified framework that treats camera-space scene flow as the central representation linking 3D structure, object motion, and camera motion. Flow4R predicts a minimal per-pixel property set-3D point position, scene flow, pose weight, and confidence-from two-view inputs using a Vision Transformer. This flow-centric formulation allows local geometry and bidirectional motion to be inferred symmetrically with a shared decoder in a single forward pass, without requiring explicit pose regressors or bundle adjustment. Trained jointly on static and dynamic datasets, Flow4R achieves state-of-the-art performance on 4D reconstruction and tracking tasks, demonstrating the effectiveness of the flow-central representation for spatiotemporal scene understanding.
tldr: The paper introduces Flow4R, a novel framework using a Vision Transformer to jointly reconstruct and track dynamic 3D scenes by predicting scene flow as a central representation, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为Flow4R的新框架，它使用Vision Transformer通过预测场景流作为中心表示来联合重建和跟踪动态3D场景，实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 动态3D场景的重建和跟踪仍然是计算机视觉领域的一个基本挑战。现有的方法通常将几何结构与运动解耦：多视角重建方法假设场景是静态的，而动态跟踪框架则依赖于显式的相机姿态估计或独立的运动模型。我们提出 Flow4R，一个统一的框架，它将相机空间场景流作为连接3D结构、物体运动和相机运动的核心表示。Flow4R 使用 Vision Transformer 从双视输入预测一个最小的逐像素属性集——3D点位置、场景流、姿态权重和置信度。这种以光流为中心的公式允许在单个前向传递中，通过共享解码器对称地推断局部几何和双向运动，而无需显式的姿态回归器或光束法平差。在静态和动态数据集上联合训练后，Flow4R 在 4D 重建和跟踪任务上取得了最先进的性能，展示了以光流为中心的表示在时空场景理解中的有效性。

title: Path Planning Optimisation for SParse, AwaRe and Cooperative Networked Aerial Robot Teams (SpArC-NARTs): Optimisation Tool and Ground Sensing Coverage Use Cases
summary: A networked aerial robot team (NART) comprises a group of agents (e.g., unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), ground control stations, etc.) interconnected by wireless links. Inter-agent connectivity, even if intermittent (i.e. sparse), enables data exchanges between agents and supports cooperative behaviours in several NART missions. It can benefit online decentralised decision-making and group resilience, particularly when prior knowledge is inaccurate or incomplete. These requirements can be accounted for in the offline mission planning stages to incentivise cooperative behaviours and improve mission efficiency during the NART deployment. This paper proposes a novel path planning tool for a Sparse, Aware, and Cooperative Networked Aerial Robot Team (SpArC-NART) in exploration missions. It simultaneously considers different levels of prior information regarding the environment, limited agent energy, sensing, and communication, as well as distinct NART constitutions. The communication model takes into account the limitations of user-defined radio technology and physical phenomena. The proposed tool aims to maximise the mission goals (e.g., finding one or multiple targets, covering the full area of the environment, etc.), while cooperating with other agents to reduce agent reporting times, increase their global situational awareness (e.g., their knowledge of the environment), and facilitate mission replanning, if required. The developed cooperation mechanism leverages soft-motion constraints and dynamic rewards based on the Value of Movement and the expected communication availability between the agents at each time step. A ground sensing coverage use case was chosen to illustrate the current capabilities of this tool.
tldr: This paper introduces a path planning tool for networked aerial robot teams (SpArC-NARTs) operating in sparse communication environments, optimizing for mission goals while considering communication constraints, agent capabilities, and cooperation through dynamic rewards. It demonstrates the tool's capabilities in a ground sensing coverage use case.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种用于在稀疏通信环境中运行的网络化空中机器人团队 (SpArC-NARTs) 的路径规划工具，该工具通过动态奖励优化任务目标，同时考虑通信约束、智能体能力和协作。 它在一个地面传感覆盖用例中演示了该工具的功能。
summary_zh: 网络化空中机器人团队(NART)由一组通过无线链路互连的智能体组成（例如，无人机(UAV)、地面控制站等）。即使是间歇性的（即稀疏的）智能体间连接，也能实现智能体之间的数据交换，并支持多种NART任务中的协同行为。这有利于在线分散决策和群体韧性，尤其是在先验知识不准确或不完整的情况下。这些需求可以在离线任务规划阶段考虑到，以激励协同行为并提高NART部署期间的任务效率。本文提出了一种新型路径规划工具，用于探索任务中的稀疏、感知和协同网络化空中机器人团队(SpArC-NART)。它同时考虑了关于环境、有限智能体能量、感知和通信以及不同NART组成的不同级别的先验信息。通信模型考虑了用户定义无线电技术和物理现象的局限性。所提出的工具旨在最大化任务目标（例如，找到一个或多个目标、覆盖环境的整个区域等），同时与其他智能体合作以减少智能体报告时间，提高它们的全局态势感知（例如，它们对环境的了解），并在需要时促进任务重新规划。所开发的协同机制利用基于运动价值和每个时间步智能体之间预期通信可用性的软运动约束和动态奖励。选择地面传感覆盖用例来说明该工具的当前能力。

title: Muscle Coactivation in the Sky: Geometry and Pareto Optimality of Energy vs. Promptness in Multirotors
summary: In robotics and human biomechanics, the tension between energy economy and kinematic readiness is well recognized; this work brings that fundamental principle to aerial multirotors. We show that the limited torque of the motors and the nonlinear aerodynamic map from rotor speed to thrust naturally give rise to the novel concept of promptness-a metric akin to dynamic aerodynamic manipulability. By treating energy consumption as a competing objective and introducing a geometric fiber-bundle formulation, we turn redundancy resolution into a principled multi-objective program on affine fibers. The use of the diffeomorphic transformation linearizing the signed-quadratic propulsion model allows us to lay the foundations for a rigorous study of the interplay between these costs. Through an illustrative case study on 4-DoF allocation on the hexarotor, we reveal that this interplay is fiber-dependent and physically shaped by hardware inequalities. For unidirectional thrusters, the feasible fibers are compact, yielding interior allocations and a short Pareto arc, while torque demands break symmetry and separate the optima. Conversely, with reversible propellers, the null space enables antagonistic rotor co-contraction that drives promptness to hardware limits, making optimal endurance and agility fundamentally incompatible in those regimes. Ultimately, rather than relying on heuristic tuning or black box algorithms to empirically improve task execution, this framework provides a foundational understanding of why and how to achieve agility through geometry-aware control allocation, offering possible guidance for vehicle design, certification metrics, and threat-aware flight operation.
tldr: The paper analyzes the trade-off between energy consumption and agility (termed "promptness") in multirotor drones, using geometric methods tooptimize control allocation and revealing fundamental limitations based on hardware constraints like unidirectional vs. reversible propellers.
tldr_zh: 该论文分析了多旋翼无人机中能量消耗和敏捷性（称为“promptness”）之间的权衡，使用几何方法优化控制分配，并揭示了基于硬件约束（如单向与可逆螺旋桨）的基本限制。
summary_zh: 在机器人学和人体生物力学中，能量经济性和运动学准备性之间的张力已被广泛认可；这项工作将这一基本原则引入到空中多旋翼飞行器中。我们证明了电机的有限扭矩以及从转子速度到推力的非线性空气动力学映射，自然地引出了一个新颖的概念——敏捷性，一种类似于动态空气动力学可操作性的指标。通过将能量消耗视为一个竞争目标，并引入几何纤维束公式，我们将冗余解析转化为仿射纤维上的有原则的多目标规划。利用微分同胚变换线性化带符号的二次推进模型，使我们能够为严格研究这些代价之间的相互作用奠定基础。通过对六旋翼飞行器上4自由度分配的说明性案例研究，我们揭示了这种相互作用是纤维依赖性的，并且受到硬件不等式的物理塑造。对于单向推进器，可行纤维是紧凑的，产生内部分配和一个短的帕累托弧，而扭矩需求打破了对称性并分离了最优值。相反，对于可逆螺旋桨，零空间能够实现拮抗性转子协同收缩，从而将敏捷性推向硬件极限，使得在这些情况下，最佳续航能力和敏捷性从根本上不相容。最终，该框架并非依赖于启发式调整或黑盒算法来经验性地改进任务执行，而是为理解如何以及为何通过几何感知控制分配来实现敏捷性提供了基础，并为车辆设计、认证指标和威胁感知飞行操作提供了可能的指导。

title: Rigidity-Based Multi-Finger Coordination for Precise In-Hand Manipulation of Force-Sensitive Objects
summary: Precise in-hand manipulation of force-sensitive objects typically requires judicious coordinated force planning as well as accurate contact force feedback and control. Unlike multi-arm platforms with gripper end effectors, multi-fingered hands rely solely on fingertip point contacts and are not able to apply pull forces, therefore poses a more challenging problem. Furthermore, calibrated torque sensors are lacking in most commercial dexterous hands, adding to the difficulty. To address these challenges, we propose a dual-layer framework for multi-finger coordination, enabling high-precision manipulation of force-sensitive objects through joint control without tactile feedback. This approach solves coordinated contact force planning by incorporating graph rigidity and force closure constraints. By employing a force-to-position mapping, the planned force trajectory is converted to a joint trajectory. We validate the framework on a custom dexterous hand, demonstrating the capability to manipulate fragile objects-including a soft yarn, a plastic cup, and a raw egg-with high precision and safety.
tldr: This paper introduces a dual-layer framework for precise in-hand manipulation of force-sensitive objects using a multi-fingered hand without tactile feedback, relying on graph rigidity and force closure constraints for coordinated force planning.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种双层框架，用于精确的力敏感物体手内操作，使用多指手，无需触觉反馈，依靠图刚性和力闭合约束进行协调的力规划。
summary_zh: 对力敏感物体的精准手内操作通常需要审慎协调的力规划以及精确的接触力反馈和控制。与使用夹持器末端执行器的多臂平台不同，多指灵巧手仅依赖于指尖点接触，无法施加拉力，因此提出了更具挑战性的问题。此外，大多数商用灵巧手缺乏校准后的扭矩传感器，进一步增加了难度。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种用于多指协同的双层框架，无需触觉反馈即可通过关节控制实现对力敏感物体的高精度操作。该方法通过结合图刚性和力封闭约束来解决协调接触力规划问题。通过采用力-位置映射，规划的力轨迹被转换为关节轨迹。我们在定制的灵巧手上验证了该框架，展示了以高精度和安全性操作易碎物体的能力，包括柔软的纱线、塑料杯和生鸡蛋。

title: RPGD: RANSAC-P3P Gradient Descent for Extrinsic Calibration in 3D Human Pose Estimation
summary: In this paper, we propose RPGD (RANSAC-P3P Gradient Descent), a human-pose-driven extrinsic calibration framework that robustly aligns MoCap-based 3D skeletal data with monocular or multi-view RGB cameras using only natural human motion. RPGD formulates extrinsic calibration as a coarse-to-fine problem tailored to human poses, combining the global robustness of RANSAC-P3P with Gradient-Descent-based refinement. We evaluate RPGD on three large-scale public 3D HPE datasets as well as on a self-collected in-the-wild dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that RPGD consistently recovers extrinsic parameters with accuracy comparable to the provided ground truth, achieving sub-pixel MPJPE reprojection error even in challenging, noisy settings. These results indicate that RPGD provides a practical and automatic solution for reliable extrinsic calibration of large-scale 3D HPE dataset collection.
tldr: The paper introduces RPGD, a novel extrinsic calibration framework for aligning MoCap-based 3D skeletal data with RGB cameras, utilizing RANSAC-P3P and gradient descent for robust and accurate calibration, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance on multiple datasets.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的外参标定框架RPGD，用于将基于MoCap的3D骨骼数据与RGB相机对齐。该方法结合了RANSAC-P3P和梯度下降，实现了稳健且准确的标定，并在多个数据集上展示了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 本文提出RPGD（RANSAC-P3P梯度下降），一种基于人体姿态驱动的外参标定框架，仅使用自然人体运动，即可稳健地将基于MoCap的3D骨骼数据与单目或多视角RGB相机对齐。RPGD将外参标定问题形式化为一个针对人体姿态的由粗到精的问题，结合RANSAC-P3P的全局鲁棒性和基于梯度下降的精细化。我们在三个大规模公开3D人体姿态估计数据集和一个自采集的实际场景数据集中评估了RPGD。实验结果表明，RPGD能够持续恢复与提供的真值精度相当的外参，即使在具有挑战性的噪声环境中也能实现亚像素级的MPJPE重投影误差。这些结果表明，RPGD为大规模3D人体姿态估计数据集采集的可靠外参标定提供了一种实用且自动化的解决方案。

title: UAV-SEAD: State Estimation Anomaly Dataset for UAVs
summary: Accurate state estimation in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) is crucial for ensuring reliable and safe operation, as anomalies occurring during mission execution may induce discrepancies between expected and observed system behaviors, thereby compromising mission success or posing potential safety hazards. It is essential to continuously monitor and detect such conditions in order to ensure a timely response and maintain system reliability. In this work, we focus on UAV state estimation anomalies and provide a large-scale real-world UAV dataset to facilitate research aimed at improving the development of anomaly detection. Unlike existing datasets that primarily rely on injected faults into simulated data, this dataset comprises 1396 real flight logs totaling over 52 hours of flight time, collected across diverse indoor and outdoor environments using a collection of PX4-based UAVs equipped with a variety of sensor configurations. The dataset comprises both normal and anomalous flights without synthetic manipulation, making it uniquely suitable for realistic anomaly detection tasks. A structured classification is proposed that categorizes UAV state estimation anomalies into four classes: mechanical and electrical, external position, global position, and altitude anomalies. These classifications reflect collective, contextual, and outlier anomalies observed in multivariate sensor data streams, including IMU, GPS, barometer, magnetometer, distance sensors, visual odometry, and optical flow, that can be found in the PX4 logging mechanism. It is anticipated that this dataset will play a key role in the development, training, and evaluation of anomaly detection and isolation systems to address the critical gap in UAV reliability research.
tldr: The paper introduces a large-scale real-world UAV dataset, UAV-SEAD, comprising both normal and anomalous flight logs, designed to facilitate research in anomaly detection for UAV state estimation, addressing a gap in existing datasets that primarily rely on simulated faults.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个大规模真实无人机数据集UAV-SEAD，包含正常和异常飞行日志，旨在促进无人机状态估计异常检测的研究，弥补了现有数据集主要依赖模拟故障的不足。
summary_zh: 在无人机（UAV）中进行精确的状态估计对于确保可靠和安全运行至关重要，因为任务执行期间发生的异常可能会导致预期和观察到的系统行为之间出现差异，从而损害任务成功或构成潜在的安全隐患。 必须持续监测和检测这些情况，以确保及时响应并维持系统可靠性。 在这项工作中，我们关注无人机状态估计异常，并提供一个大规模的真实世界无人机数据集，以促进旨在改进异常检测的研究。 与主要依赖于将故障注入模拟数据的现有数据集不同，该数据集包含 1396 个真实飞行日志，总飞行时间超过 52 小时，这些日志是使用基于 PX4 的无人机集合在各种室内和室外环境中收集的，这些无人机配备了各种传感器配置。 该数据集包含正常和异常飞行，没有经过合成操作，这使其非常适合真实的异常检测任务。 提出了一种结构化分类方法，该方法将无人机状态估计异常分为四类：机械和电气异常、外部位置异常、全局位置异常和高度异常。 这些分类反映了在多元传感器数据流中观察到的集体、上下文和离群异常，这些数据流包括可以在 PX4 日志记录机制中找到的 IMU、GPS、气压计、磁力计、距离传感器、视觉里程计和光流。 预计该数据集将在异常检测和隔离系统的开发、训练和评估中发挥关键作用，以解决无人机可靠性研究中的关键差距。

title: Modeling and Optimizing the Provisioning of Exhaustible Capabilities for Simultaneous Task Allocation and Scheduling
summary: Deploying heterogeneous robot teams to accomplish multiple tasks over extended time horizons presents significant computational challenges for task allocation and planning. In this paper, we present a comprehensive, time-extended, offline heterogeneous multi-robot task allocation framework, TRAITS, which we believe to be the first that can cope with the provisioning of exhaustible traits under battery and temporal constraints. Specifically, we introduce a nonlinear programming-based trait distribution module that can optimize the trait-provisioning rate of coalitions to yield feasible and time-efficient solutions. TRAITS provides a more accurate feasibility assessment and estimation of task execution times and makespan by leveraging trait-provisioning rates while optimizing battery consumption -- an advantage that state-of-the-art frameworks lack. We evaluate TRAITS against two state-of-the-art frameworks, with results demonstrating its advantage in satisfying complex trait and battery requirements while remaining computationally tractable.
tldr: This paper presents TRAITS, a nonlinear programming-based framework for multi-robot task allocation and scheduling that optimizes the provision of exhaustible capabilities under battery and temporal constraints, outperforming state-of-the-art frameworks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于非线性规划的多机器人任务分配和调度框架TRAITS，该框架优化了在电池和时间约束下耗尽能力的供应，优于最先进的框架。
summary_zh: 在较长时间范围内部署异构机器人团队以完成多个任务，对任务分配和规划提出了巨大的计算挑战。 在本文中，我们提出了一个全面的、时间扩展的、离线异构多机器人任务分配框架TRAITS，我们认为这是第一个可以处理在电池和时间约束下耗尽性特征供给的框架。 具体而言，我们引入了一个基于非线性规划的特征分配模块，该模块可以优化联盟的特征供给率，以产生可行且时间高效的解决方案。 TRAITS通过利用特征供给率同时优化电池消耗，从而提供更准确的可行性评估以及任务执行时间和完工时间（makespan）的估计，这是现有最优框架所缺乏的优势。我们将TRAITS与两个现有最优框架进行评估，结果表明其在满足复杂特征和电池需求方面具有优势，同时保持了计算上的可处理性。

title: Detection of On-Ground Chestnuts Using Artificial Intelligence Toward Automated Picking
summary: Traditional mechanized chestnut harvesting is too costly for small producers, non-selective, and prone to damaging nuts. Accurate, reliable detection of chestnuts on the orchard floor is crucial for developing low-cost, vision-guided automated harvesting technology. However, developing a reliable chestnut detection system faces challenges in complex environments with shading, varying natural light conditions, and interference from weeds, fallen leaves, stones, and other foreign on-ground objects, which have remained unaddressed. This study collected 319 images of chestnuts on the orchard floor, containing 6524 annotated chestnuts. A comprehensive set of 29 state-of-the-art real-time object detectors, including 14 in the YOLO (v11-13) and 15 in the RT-DETR (v1-v4) families at varied model scales, was systematically evaluated through replicated modeling experiments for chestnut detection. Experimental results show that the YOLOv12m model achieves the best mAP@0.5 of 95.1% among all the evaluated models, while the RT-DETRv2-R101 was the most accurate variant among RT-DETR models, with mAP@0.5 of 91.1%. In terms of mAP@[0.5:0.95], the YOLOv11x model achieved the best accuracy of 80.1%. All models demonstrate significant potential for real-time chestnut detection, and YOLO models outperformed RT-DETR models in terms of both detection accuracy and inference, making them better suited for on-board deployment. Both the dataset and software programs in this study have been made publicly available at https://github.com/AgFood-Sensing-and-Intelligence-Lab/ChestnutDetection.
tldr: This paper evaluates 29 state-of-the-art object detectors for on-ground chestnut detection, finding that YOLO models outperform RT-DETR models and demonstrating significant potential for real-time automated harvesting.
tldr_zh: 本文评估了29种最先进的目标检测器在地面栗子检测中的性能，发现YOLO模型优于RT-DETR模型，并展示了实时自动化收获的巨大潜力。
summary_zh: 传统机械化栗子采摘对于小型生产者来说成本过高，缺乏选择性，并且容易损坏栗子。在果园地面上准确、可靠地检测栗子对于开发低成本、视觉引导的自动化采摘技术至关重要。然而，开发可靠的栗子检测系统面临着复杂环境中的挑战，包括阴影、变化的光照条件以及来自杂草、落叶、石块和其他地面异物的干扰，这些挑战尚未得到解决。本研究收集了果园地面上的319张栗子图像，包含6524个带标注的栗子。通过重复建模实验，对包含14个YOLO（v11-13）系列和15个RT-DETR（v1-v4）系列的不同模型规模的29个最先进的实时目标检测器进行了系统评估，用于栗子检测。实验结果表明，在所有评估模型中，YOLOv12m模型实现了最佳的mAP@0.5，达到95.1%，而RT-DETRv2-R101是RT-DETR模型中最准确的变体，mAP@0.5为91.1%。在mAP@[0.5:0.95]方面，YOLOv11x模型达到了80.1%的最佳精度。所有模型都展现出实时栗子检测的巨大潜力，并且YOLO模型在检测精度和推理速度方面均优于RT-DETR模型，使其更适合车载部署。本研究中的数据集和软件程序已在https://github.com/AgFood-Sensing-and-Intelligence-Lab/ChestnutDetection上公开可用。

title: An effective Genetic Programming Hyper-Heuristic for Uncertain Agile Satellite Scheduling
summary: This paper investigates a novel problem, namely the Uncertain Agile Earth Observation Satellite Scheduling Problem (UAEOSSP). Unlike the static AEOSSP, it takes into account a range of uncertain factors (e.g., task profit, resource consumption, and task visibility) in order to reflect the reality that the actual information is inherently unknown beforehand. An effective Genetic Programming Hyper-Heuristic (GPHH) is designed to automate the generation of scheduling policies. The evolved scheduling policies can be utilized to adjust plans in real time and perform exceptionally well. Experimental results demonstrate that evolved scheduling policies significantly outperform both well-designed Look-Ahead Heuristics (LAHs) and Manually Designed Heuristics (MDHs). Specifically, the policies generated by GPHH achieve an average improvement of 5.03% compared to LAHs and 8.14% compared to MDHs.
tldr: This paper introduces a Genetic Programming Hyper-Heuristic (GPHH) for the Uncertain Agile Earth Observation Satellite Scheduling Problem (UAEOSSP), showing significant performance improvements over existing heuristics.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于不确定敏捷地球观测卫星调度问题（UAEOSSP）的遗传编程超启发式算法（GPHH），并在性能上显著优于现有启发式算法。
summary_zh: 本文研究了一个新的问题，即不确定性敏捷对地观测卫星调度问题（UAEOSSP）。与静态AEOSSP不同，它考虑了一系列不确定因素（例如，任务收益、资源消耗和任务可见性），以反映实际信息事先固有的未知性。设计了一种有效的遗传编程超启发式算法（GPHH）来自动生成调度策略。所演化的调度策略可用于实时调整计划，并表现出卓越的性能。实验结果表明，所演化的调度策略显著优于精心设计的前瞻启发式算法（LAH）和手动设计启发式算法（MDH）。具体而言，GPHH生成的策略与LAH相比平均提升了5.03%，与MDH相比平均提升了8.14%。

title: ARport: An Augmented Reality System for Markerless Image-Guided Port Placement in Robotic Surgery
summary: Purpose: Precise port placement is a critical step in robot-assisted surgery, where port configuration influences both visual access to the operative field and instrument maneuverability. To bridge the gap between preoperative planning and intraoperative execution, we present ARport, an augmented reality (AR) system that automatically maps pre-planned trocar layouts onto the patient's body surface, providing intuitive spatial guidance during surgical preparation. Methods: ARport, implemented on an optical see-through head-mounted display (OST-HMD), operates without any external sensors or markers, simplifying setup and enhancing workflow integration. It reconstructs the operative scene from RGB, depth, and pose data captured by the OST-HMD, extracts the patient's body surface using a foundation model, and performs surface-based markerless registration to align preoperative anatomical models to the extracted patient's body surface, enabling in-situ visualization of planned trocar layouts. A demonstration video illustrating the overall workflow is available online. Results: In full-scale human-phantom experiments, ARport accurately overlaid pre-planned trocar sites onto the physical phantom, achieving consistent spatial correspondence between virtual plans and real anatomy. Conclusion: ARport provides a fully marker-free and hardware-minimal solution for visualizing preoperative trocar plans directly on the patient's body surface. The system facilitates efficient intraoperative setup and demonstrates potential for seamless integration into routine clinical workflows.
tldr: The paper introduces ARport, an augmented reality system that uses a head-mounted display and markerless registration to project pre-planned trocar layouts onto a patient's body surface for robot-assisted surgery, aiming to improve port placement accuracy and workflow efficiency.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为ARport的增强现实系统，该系统使用头戴式显示器和无标记注册将预先计划的套管针布局投影到患者体表，用于机器人辅助手术，旨在提高端口放置的准确性和工作流程效率。
summary_zh: 目的：精确的穿刺口定位是机器人辅助手术的关键步骤，穿刺口配置会影响手术视野的可视化以及器械的可操作性。为了弥合术前规划与术中执行之间的差距，我们提出ARport，这是一种增强现实（AR）系统，可自动将预先规划的套管针布局映射到患者的身体表面，在手术准备期间提供直观的空间指导。方法：ARport采用光学透视头戴式显示器（OST-HMD）实现，无需任何外部传感器或标记物即可运行，从而简化了设置并增强了工作流程集成。它从OST-HMD捕获的RGB、深度和姿态数据中重建手术场景，使用基础模型提取患者身体表面，并执行基于表面的无标记配准，将术前解剖模型与提取的患者身体表面对齐，从而实现对计划套管针布局的原位可视化。一段演示整体工作流程的视频已在线提供。结果：在全尺寸人体模型实验中，ARport准确地将预先规划的套管针位置叠加到物理模型上，实现了虚拟计划和真实解剖结构之间一致的空间对应关系。结论：ARport提供了一种完全无标记且硬件极简的解决方案，用于直接在患者身体表面可视化术前套管针计划。该系统有助于高效的术中设置，并展示了无缝集成到常规临床工作流程中的潜力。

title: GeoFusionLRM: Geometry-Aware Self-Correction for Consistent 3D Reconstruction
summary: Single-image 3D reconstruction with large reconstruction models (LRMs) has advanced rapidly, yet reconstructions often exhibit geometric inconsistencies and misaligned details that limit fidelity. We introduce GeoFusionLRM, a geometry-aware self-correction framework that leverages the model's own normal and depth predictions to refine structural accuracy. Unlike prior approaches that rely solely on features extracted from the input image, GeoFusionLRM feeds back geometric cues through a dedicated transformer and fusion module, enabling the model to correct errors and enforce consistency with the conditioning image. This design improves the alignment between the reconstructed mesh and the input views without additional supervision or external signals. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GeoFusionLRM achieves sharper geometry, more consistent normals, and higher fidelity than state-of-the-art LRM baselines.
tldr: GeoFusionLRM uses a geometry-aware self-correction framework to improve the consistency and accuracy of 3D reconstructions from single images by feeding geometric cues back into the model, achieving state-of-the-art results without extra supervision.
tldr_zh: GeoFusionLRM 使用一种几何感知的自我校正框架，通过将几何提示反馈给模型来提高单图像 3D 重建的一致性和准确性，无需额外监督即达到最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 使用大型重建模型 (LRM) 的单图像 3D 重建技术发展迅速，但重建结果通常表现出几何不一致性和细节错位，限制了其保真度。 我们引入了 GeoFusionLRM，一种几何感知自校正框架，它利用模型自身预测的法线和深度来精细化结构精度。与先前仅依赖于从输入图像提取特征的方法不同，GeoFusionLRM 通过专用Transformer和融合模块反馈几何线索，使模型能够校正误差并强制与条件图像保持一致。这种设计提高了重建网格与输入视图之间的对齐，而无需额外的监督或外部信号。 广泛的实验表明，GeoFusionLRM 比最先进的 LRM 基线实现了更清晰的几何形状、更一致的法线和更高的保真度。

title: A Penalty Approach for Differentiation Through Black-Box Quadratic Programming Solvers
summary: Differentiating through the solution of a quadratic program (QP) is a central problem in differentiable optimization. Most existing approaches differentiate through the Karush--Kuhn--Tucker (KKT) system, but their computational cost and numerical robustness can degrade at scale. To address these limitations, we propose dXPP, a penalty-based differentiation framework that decouples QP solving from differentiation. In the solving step (forward pass), dXPP is solver-agnostic and can leverage any black-box QP solver. In the differentiation step (backward pass), we map the solution to a smooth approximate penalty problem and implicitly differentiate through it, requiring only the solution of a much smaller linear system in the primal variables. This approach bypasses the difficulties inherent in explicit KKT differentiation and significantly improves computational efficiency and robustness. We evaluate dXPP on various tasks, including randomly generated QPs, large-scale sparse projection problems, and a real-world multi-period portfolio optimization task. Empirical results demonstrate that dXPP is competitive with KKT-based differentiation methods and achieves substantial speedups on large-scale problems.
tldr: The paper introduces dXPP, a penalty-based differentiation framework for quadratic programs that offers improved computational efficiency and robustness compared to KKT-based methods, especially for large-scale problems.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了dXPP，一种基于惩罚的二次规划微分框架，与基于KKT的方法相比，它提供了更高的计算效率和鲁棒性，尤其是在大规模问题上。
summary_zh: 通过求解二次规划(QP)进行微分是可微优化中的一个核心问题。大多数现有方法通过Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT)系统进行微分，但它们的计算成本和数值鲁棒性可能会随着规模的扩大而降低。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了dXPP，一个基于惩罚的微分框架，它将QP求解与微分解耦。在求解步骤（前向传递）中，dXPP与求解器无关，并且可以利用任何黑盒QP求解器。在微分步骤（反向传递）中，我们将解映射到一个平滑的近似惩罚问题，并通过它进行隐式微分，只需要求解一个规模小得多的基于原始变量的线性系统。该方法绕过了显式KKT微分固有的困难，并显著提高了计算效率和鲁棒性。我们在各种任务上评估了dXPP，包括随机生成的QP，大规模稀疏投影问题，以及一个实际的多周期投资组合优化任务。实验结果表明，dXPP可以与基于KKT的微分方法相媲美，并在大规模问题上实现了显著的加速。

title: KoopGen: Koopman Generator Networks for Representing and Predicting Dynamical Systems with Continuous Spectra
summary: Representing and predicting high-dimensional and spatiotemporally chaotic dynamical systems remains a fundamental challenge in dynamical systems and machine learning. Although data-driven models can achieve accurate short-term forecasts, they often lack stability, interpretability, and scalability in regimes dominated by broadband or continuous spectra. Koopman-based approaches provide a principled linear perspective on nonlinear dynamics, but existing methods rely on restrictive finite-dimensional assumptions or explicit spectral parameterizations that degrade in high-dimensional settings. Against these issues, we introduce KoopGen, a generator-based neural Koopman framework that models dynamics through a structured, state-dependent representation of Koopman generators. By exploiting the intrinsic Cartesian decomposition into skew-adjoint and self-adjoint components, KoopGen separates conservative transport from irreversible dissipation while enforcing exact operator-theoretic constraints during learning. Across systems ranging from nonlinear oscillators to high-dimensional chaotic and spatiotemporal dynamics, KoopGen improves prediction accuracy and stability, while clarifying which components of continuous-spectrum dynamics admit interpretable and learnable representations.
tldr: KoopGen is a new neural Koopman framework that uses a generator-based approach to model and predict high-dimensional chaotic dynamical systems, improving prediction accuracy, stability, and interpretability by separating conservative transport from irreversible dissipation.
tldr_zh: KoopGen是一种新型神经 Koopman 框架，它使用基于生成器的方法来建模和预测高维混沌动力系统，通过分离保守传输和不可逆耗散，提高了预测精度、稳定性和可解释性。
summary_zh: 表示及预测高维时空混沌动力学系统仍然是动力系统和机器学习领域的一项根本性挑战。尽管数据驱动模型可以实现准确的短期预测，但它们在高带宽或连续谱主导的区域常常缺乏稳定性、可解释性和可扩展性。基于Koopman算子的方法为非线性动力学提供了一种原则性的线性视角，但现有方法依赖于约束性的有限维假设或显式谱参数化，这些假设或参数化在高维环境中会退化。针对这些问题，我们引入了KoopGen，一种基于生成器的神经Koopman框架，它通过Koopman生成器的结构化、状态依赖表示来对动力学进行建模。通过利用内在的笛卡尔分解为斜伴随和自伴随分量，KoopGen分离了守恒输运和不可逆耗散，同时在学习过程中强制执行精确的算子理论约束。 在从非线性振荡器到高维混沌和时空动力学的各种系统中，KoopGen提高了预测精度和稳定性，同时阐明了连续谱动力学的哪些分量可以具有可解释和可学习的表示。

title: UniST-Pred: A Robust Unified Framework for Spatio-Temporal Traffic Forecasting in Transportation Networks Under Disruptions
summary: Spatio-temporal traffic forecasting is a core component of intelligent transportation systems, supporting various downstream tasks such as signal control and network-level traffic management. In real-world deployments, forecasting models must operate under structural and observational uncertainties, conditions that are rarely considered in model design. Recent approaches achieve strong short-term predictive performance by tightly coupling spatial and temporal modeling, often at the cost of increased complexity and limited modularity. In contrast, efficient time-series models capture long-range temporal dependencies without relying on explicit network structure. We propose UniST-Pred, a unified spatio-temporal forecasting framework that first decouples temporal modeling from spatial representation learning, then integrates both through adaptive representation-level fusion. To assess robustness of the proposed approach, we construct a dataset based on an agent-based, microscopic traffic simulator (MATSim) and evaluate UniST-Pred under severe network disconnection scenarios. Additionally, we benchmark UniST-Pred on standard traffic prediction datasets, demonstrating its competitive performance against existing well-established models despite a lightweight design. The results illustrate that UniST-Pred maintains strong predictive performance across both real-world and simulated datasets, while also yielding interpretable spatio-temporal representations under infrastructure disruptions. The source code and the generated dataset are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/UniST-Pred-EF27
tldr: The paper introduces UniST-Pred, a unified spatio-temporal traffic forecasting framework robust to network disruptions, achieving competitive performance with a lightweight design across real-world and simulated datasets.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了UniST-Pred，一个统一的时空交通预测框架，对网络中断具有鲁棒性，并通过轻量级设计在真实和模拟数据集中实现了有竞争力的性能。
summary_zh: 时空交通预测是智能交通系统的核心组成部分，为信号控制和网络级交通管理等各种下游任务提供支持。在实际部署中，预测模型必须在结构性和观测不确定性下运行，而这些条件在模型设计中很少被考虑。最近的方法通过紧密耦合空间和时间建模来获得强大的短期预测性能，但通常以增加复杂性和限制模块化为代价。相反，高效的时间序列模型能够捕获长程时间依赖性，而无需依赖显式的网络结构。我们提出了UniST-Pred，一个统一的时空预测框架，它首先将时间建模与空间表征学习解耦，然后通过自适应的表征级融合将两者整合。为了评估所提出方法的鲁棒性，我们基于一个基于代理的微观交通模拟器（MATSim）构建了一个数据集，并在严重的网络断连场景下评估UniST-Pred。此外，我们在标准交通预测数据集上对UniST-Pred进行了基准测试，证明了它尽管设计轻巧，但与现有的成熟模型相比具有竞争力的性能。结果表明，UniST-Pred在真实世界和模拟数据集上都保持了强大的预测性能，同时在基础设施中断情况下也能产生可解释的时空表示。源代码和生成的数据集可在https://anonymous.4open.science/r/UniST-Pred-EF27 获取。

title: Human-Aligned Evaluation of a Pixel-wise DNN Color Constancy Model
summary: We previously investigated color constancy in photorealistic virtual reality (VR) and developed a Deep Neural Network (DNN) that predicts reflectance from rendered images. Here, we combine both approaches to compare and study a model and human performance with respect to established color constancy mechanisms: local surround, maximum flux and spatial mean. Rather than evaluating the model against physical ground truth, model performance was assessed using the same achromatic object selection task employed in the human experiments. The model, a ResNet based U-Net from our previous work, was pre-trained on rendered images to predict surface reflectance. We then applied transfer learning, fine-tuning only the network's decoder on images from the baseline VR condition. To parallel the human experiment, the model's output was used to perform the same achromatic object selection task across all conditions. Results show a strong correspondence between the model and human behavior. Both achieved high constancy under baseline conditions and showed similar, condition-dependent performance declines when the local surround or spatial mean color cues were removed.
tldr: This paper compares a DNN color constancy model against human performance in a VR environment, finding a strong correspondence when assessing established color constancy mechanisms.
tldr_zh: 本文在虚拟现实环境中将DNN颜色恒常性模型与人类表现进行比较，发现在评估已建立的颜色恒常性机制时，两者表现出很强的对应关系。
summary_zh: 我们先前研究了照片级真实虚拟现实(VR)中的颜色恒常性，并开发了一种深度神经网络(DNN)，用于从渲染图像中预测反射率。在此，我们将两种方法结合起来，以比较和研究模型和人类在已建立的颜色恒常性机制（局部包围、最大通量和空间平均）方面的表现。不与物理真值进行评估，而是使用与人类实验中相同的消色对象选择任务来评估模型性能。该模型是我们之前工作中的基于ResNet的U-Net，经过预训练，可以预测渲染图像的表面反射率。然后，我们应用了迁移学习，仅在基线VR条件下的图像上微调网络的解码器。为了与人类实验并行，我们使用模型的输出来执行所有条件下的相同消色对象选择任务。结果表明模型和人类行为之间存在很强的对应关系。两者在基线条件下都实现了高度的恒常性，并且在移除局部包围或空间平均颜色线索时，都表现出相似的、依赖于条件的性能下降。
